0.q: MaGRAS51 interacts with Nodulation Signaling Pathway 1 (MaNSP1) and jointly binds to the promoter of the Nodule Inception (MaNIN) gene to activate its expression.

2.q: the expression of MaENOD11 was markedly enhanced in OE-MaGRAS51 hairy roots and suppressed in RNAi-MaGRAS51 hairy roots

3.q: MaGRAS51 was highly induced by salt stress during nodulation and activated the expression of MaNIN by directly binding to its promoter independently.

4.q: Overexpression of MaGRAS51 significantly increased nodule number, whereas its knockdown resulted in a marked reduction in nodulation, confirming its essential role in nodule formation.

5.q: NSP1 directly binds to and activates the expression of the NIN gene, representing a central regulatory pathway in the symbiosis signaling network of leguminous plants.

6.q: expression levels of MaNIN were significantly upregulated in OE-MaGRAS51 hairy roots and downregulated in RNAi-MaGRAS51 hairy roots compared to the empty vector control
